The long walk, part 2

The second week began in fog so thick that the cliff edge existed mostly as a rumour. We navigated by the sound of the sea and the occasional apologetic sheep.

Days eight to ten

Days eight to ten covered the estuary crossings, where the tide tables mattered more than the map. We learned to read the sandbanks the hard way, which is to say wetly.

Days eleven to fourteen

Days eleven to fourteen brought the high moorland section, where the path forgets it is a coast path and goes inland to sulk among the gorse.
